acceptance_criteria: Schema file committed in-repo at a path consistent with polyphony-workflow-author conventions
Schema captures `topics[]`, `context`, `budget_hint`, `archive_scope` fields with documented types
Short README or in-file comments document the shape and its role as the architect ↔ research sub-workflow contract
Schema validates against at least one hand-authored example payload (committed alongside)
description:
Author the `research_needs` payload schema that the architect emits and the research sub-workflow consumes. This is the stable contract that Issue #3136 binds to — it must land first so downstream work has something concrete to reference.

**Shape (minimum):**
- `topics[]` — required questions or research areas (string list, non-empty)
- `context` — originating work-item id + relevant plan excerpt
- `budget_hint` — `cheap` | `extended` (this issue only honors `cheap`)
- `archive_scope` — optional path/tag constraints for the archive lookup

**Location:** Land under [.polyphony-config/](\.polyphony-config/) or a workflow-adjacent path consistent with polyphony-workflow-author conventions. Document the shape in a short README adjacent to the schema file.

**No code consumers required in this task** — task-2 wires the sub-workflow to consume it, task-3 wires the agent.
title: Define `research_needs` payload schema

acceptance_criteria: New workflow YAML committed under [workflows/](workflows/) with a name consistent with the three-vocabulary rule
Workflow accepts a `research_needs` payload conforming to the task-1 schema
Workflow routes to the `research-assistant` agent and surfaces its output as a findings Markdown document
`polyphony validate` (or equivalent in-repo workflow validator) passes on the new YAML
Build passes with zero errors and warnings
description:
Author a new conductor workflow YAML under [workflows/](workflows/) that accepts a `research_needs` payload (per the schema from task-1), routes to the `research-assistant` agent (added in task-3), and returns a synthesized findings Markdown document.

**Conventions to follow:**
- polyphony-workflow-author skill: shell-out idiom, three-vocabulary rule (events / state names / categories), established helper scripts.
- conductor-mechanics: agent output access, route conditions, model selection (cheap tier for `research-assistant`).

**Scope:** Skeleton only — single agent leg (no sufficiency check, no escalation, no archivist write). Those land in #3134 / #3135.

**Invocability:** Must be invokable from the harness (see [tests/harness/](tests/harness/)) — task-3 adds the actual scenario; this task must leave the workflow in a state where the harness can drive it.
title: Research sub-workflow YAML skeleton

acceptance_criteria: `research-assistant` agent prompt and agent-guidance entry committed; cheap tier wired via profile
Agent guidance documents the archive-only constraint and the chosen archive surface
Harness scenario under [tests/harness/scenarios/](tests/harness/scenarios/) drives the sub-workflow end-to-end with a scripted `FakeProvider`
Scenario asserts the returned findings document shape (Markdown, per-topic sections, sources)
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; new and existing tests pass
description:
Author the `research-assistant` agent prompt and agent-guidance entry, and add a harness scenario that drives the sub-workflow from task-2 end-to-end with a scripted `FakeProvider`.

**Agent constraints:**
- Cheap model tier per [.polyphony-config/profile.yaml](.polyphony-config/profile.yaml).
- **Archive-only** — no live web/MCP/tool calls wired in this pass. Sources are limited to whatever archive surface the repo currently exposes (document the chosen surface in the agent guidance).
- Output: a single synthesized findings Markdown document with a simple, documented shape (e.g., `# Findings`, per-topic sections, sources list).

**Harness scenario (under [tests/harness/scenarios/](tests/harness/scenarios/)):**
- Drive the sub-workflow with a representative `research_needs` payload (the example from task-1).
- Use a scripted `FakeProvider` to simulate the assistant's response.
- Assert the workflow returns a findings document of the expected shape.

**Out of scope:** Sufficiency check, `deep-researcher` escalation, archivist write — those are #3134 / #3135.
title: `research-assistant` agent (archive-only) + harness scenario
